channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19360] lustre-initialization: IPv6 "Failed to get NID for server"
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/a4e964e7-90a3-41f7-8ab8-d71f7341e301

lustre-initialization failed with the following error from the test scripts:

2025-09-11T23:22:16 CMD: onyx-149vm11 mkdir -p /mnt/lustre-mds2; mount -t lustre -o localrecov  /dev/mapper/mds2_flakey /mnt/lustre-mds2
2025-09-11T23:22:16 onyx-149vm11: mount.lustre: mount /dev/mapper/mds2_flakey at /mnt/lustre-mds2 failed: Invalid argument

and the following messages on the server console:

[  173.892382] LDISKFS-fs (dm-3): mounted filesystem with ordered data mode. Opts: errors=remount-ro
[  173.903347] Lustre: lustre-MDT0001: mounting server target with '-t lustre' deprecated, use '-t lustre_tgt'
[  173.924933] LDISKFS-fs (dm-3): mounted filesystem with ordered data mode. Opts: user_xattr,acl,no_mbcache,nodelalloc
[  174.246298] LustreError: 13213:0:(tgt_mount.c:1259:server_lsi2mti()) Failed to get NID for server lustre-MDT0001, please check whether the target is specifed with improper --servicenode or --network options.
[  174.251261] LustreError: 13213:0:(tgt_mount.c:2460:server_fill_super()) Unable to start targets: -22
[  174.253035] LustreError: 13213:0:(tgt_mount.c:1954:server_put_super()) no obd lustre-MDT0001
[  174.254562] LustreError: 13213:0:(tgt_mount.c:117:server_deregister_mount()) lustre-MDT0001 not registered
[  174.257151] LustreError: 13213:0:(obd_class.h:479:obd_check_dev()) Device 1 not setup
[  174.286001] Lustre: server umount lustre-MDT0001 complete
[  174.287088] LustreError: 13213:0:(super25.c:181:lustre_fill_super()) llite: Unable to mount <unknown>: rc = -22
[  235.058774] Autotest: Auster exited unexpectedly (lustre-reviews_review-dne-ipv6_116451.64)

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/116451 - 4.18.0-553.71.1.el8_10.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/116451 - 4.18.0-553.71.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

A number of IPv6 sessions are failing during initialization

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
lustre-initialization lustre-initialization - "auster exited unexpectedly"
item.summary: lustre-initialization: IPv6 "Failed to get NID for server"

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: The "Failed to get NID for server" failure didn't occur recently. Let's close this ticket.

Some new occurrences:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/ee95423f-dcff-47c4-a306-1e0f79bb16a4
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/c06616a4-0bae-4e1c-aef6-7c2374ca0cbb
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/eee864b3-c8d8-4514-9d66-20b4ab42b846
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/07cc73c8-76d2-45e6-9b68-9c94b6dbcedc

I looked the 4 links and 3 concern this bug, but they are old.
I checked test results again and found no issue concerning this ticket after Oct 15. I am not sure if this bug is fixed in some way, or if it is hidden by one of the many other bugs we have with "lustre-initialization". Since other bugs (new, being solved and solved) concern also IPv6, code has changed and will. So, I don't think I should put effort trying to reproduce this bug using an old version. I will watch if there is a new occurrence.
Thank you.

Oh, I did two mistakes. For IPv6, I used "IP version" filter and did not think about review-dne-ipv6 sessions. And my script that checked other runs, was using same filename overwriting logs with same VM node…
Anyway, thank you yujian. I will fix my script and study that.

Hi cbordage,
Here are some records in the last week:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/30217488-c1b6-4e5a-9cc3-e91d246ffb67
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/6336f06e-0d6b-4868-853d-09cf6a1bcc3c
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/d1ef7f05-9ce2-456b-98c6-6b07fa828781
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/ec8db6c8-93e5-459a-b734-26e1b8a2c8be

The debugging code cannot be merged into master, it wasn't mean to.
My problem is I cannot reproduce the bug, and I cannot find any recent occurrence in autotest…
yujian What are the records you are referring to? I cannot find one having this specific issue. In the last two weeks, there is only one record failing and having IPv6 version, and it is linked to another ticket. Even for IPv4 version, there is no issue having "Failed to get NID"… Did I miss something?
Cyril is the debugging code merged into master?
102 records found in the last two weeks on master branch.

I was able to reproduce it easily, simply by running the tests with IPv6 several times.
I was on a lead, and now I have a code that is instrumented to check my theory, but now I cannot reproduce it anymore… It is not because of my debugging changes, since they happen after the error messages appears.

Here is the frequency of lustre-initialization failures:
37 2025-09-02
34 2025-09-03
11 2025-09-04
2 2025-09-05
1 2025-09-07
21 2025-09-08
16 2025-09-10
17 2025-09-11
8 2025-09-12
3 2025-09-14
8 2025-09-15
10 2025-09-16
4 2025-09-17
7 2025-09-18
1 2025-09-19
6 2025-09-20
1 2025-09-22
27 2025-09-24
12 2025-09-25
1 2025-09-26
4 2025-09-27
3 2025-09-28
6 2025-09-30
2 2025-10-01
3 2025-10-02
That includes other issues than the one described here and leading to lustre-initialization failure.
Did something change on the test env?

I need to look at those patches again for LU-18041. Mikhail pointed out that code is about getting a marker for a node not so much UUIDs. I have thought about it for some time after the discussion and I realized the setup in  client_obd_setup() does the same thing as client_import_add_nids_to_conn().  Some cleanup can be done there.

Ah I see why it works for me. My IPv6 NID is in short form so it avoids these issues.

See my comment in LU-18041, but it looks like this issue may be behind us, and now there are timeouts in some cases when the client is mounting due to a large UUID, rather than when the OST is mounting.

I suspect this is due to the work Mikhail has been doing. Maybe LU-19253?

I just tested IPv6 locally and it works for me. Something in maloo setup is not right?

Chris Horn can you mount lustre with IPv6?

This failure is blocking patch review testing for https://review.whamcloud.com/61337 ("LU-19323 tests: add more sanity-lnet subtests into IPv6 except list").
lustre-initialization failed: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/2a873d77-6e18-4c62-9a8e-bb9c4b16d237

hornc, I don't see any reporting of node IP addresses during normal startup, only in sanity-lnet.sh? While I don't think this is a node addressing issue (there are many sessions that are running), I haven't done any investigation on whether there is a correlation between failed IPv6 sessions and the nodes they are running on.

It looks like lustre-initialization has failed 9/64 ~= 12% of test sessions for review-dne-ipv6 in the past week. In comparison, review-dne-part-1 failed 4/221 ~= 2% of lustre-initialization sessions in the same time period, so it definitely seems related to IPv6.

It looks like the problem has happened multiple times with MDT0001 and OST0000. I don't see any easy way to debug this further, since the failed lustre-initialization sessions do not collect/save the Lustre debug logs. I will file a separate ticket for that.
